title: HS/OS (V) China’s Artistic Diaspora: Late Imperial to Contemporary (1900-2020s) (Fraser)
organisation: Institute of East Asian Art History
further_info: Recommended Reading Course Readings: Croizier, Ralph C. “The Japanese Connection.” In Art and Revolution in Modern China: The Lingnan (Cantonese) School of Painting, 1906–1951, 25–61. Berkeley: University of California Press, 1988. Bao, Hongwei. “The New Generation: Contemporary Chinese Art in the Diaspora.” Journal of Contemporary Chinese Art 9, n. 1-2 (July 1, 2022): 3–17. Gao, Minglu. “Strategies of Survival in the Third Space.” In Inside Out: New Chinese Art, ed. Gao Minglu, 183–189. Berkeley: University of California Press, 1998. Hall, Stuart. “Cultural Identity and Diaspora.” In Colonial Discourse and Post-Colonial Theory, 1st Edition. Routledge, 1994. Juneja, Monica. “Introduction.” In Can Art History Be Made Global?, 11–40. Berlin/Boston: Walter de Gruyter GmbH, 2023. Karlstrom, Paul. “A Modernist Painter's Journey in America” In Yun Gee: Poetry, Writings, Art, Memories. Seattle and Pasadena, CA: University of Washington Press and Pasadena Museum of California Art, 2003. Pan, Gaojie. “Art Practices of the Chinese Women Diaspora: On Cultural Identity and Gender Modernity.” Journal of Contemporary Chinese Art 9, n. 1 (2022): 45–68. Pejčochová, Michaela. “The Transformation of Chinese Painting in the Past Two Centuries.” In Masters of 20th-Century Chinese Ink Painting from the Collections of the National Gallery in Prague, edited by Michaela Pejčochová and Tomáš Winter, 10–23. Prague: National Gallery in Prague, 2008.
